summary: sea-bound-siren postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and npm tokens to hardcoded attacker IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js runs a /bin/sh -c reconnaissance script that reads AWS environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SESSION_TOKEN), dumps /root/.npmrc, /home/node/.npmrc and /app/.npmrc, calls `aws sts get-caller-identity` and enumerates `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` / `get-secret-value` across regions, and greps env for AWS_/FLAG/HTB tokens. Output is base64-encoded and PUT to hardcoded endpoints http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201 and http://127.0.0.1:30782. A shouldRun() gate skips execution when the npm registry matches Chinese mirrors/scanners (npmmirror, tencent, taobao, huawei, cnpm, ustc, nijin, hscan) or the cwd is under /tmp/, and only fires when the hostname looks like a 12-character container hex ID with cwd /app/node_modules or /home/node — anti-analysis evasion targeting real CI/production environments while hiding from registry-side scanners. Despite a CTF/HTB cover story, the AWS Secrets Manager enumeration and npmrc dump harm any installer whose environment satisfies the gate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
